heading: Prohibited obligations and expenditures
label: 1517 Prohibited obligations and expenditures
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised SectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)1517(a)31:665(h).R.S. § 3679(h), (i)(2)(related to (h)); (1st par.), ; , ; restated , .Mar. 3, 1905, ch. 1484, § 433 Stat. 1257Feb. 27, 1906, ch. 510, § 334 Stat. 48Sept. 6, 1950, ch. 896, § 121164 Stat. 7681517(b)31:665(i)(2)(related to (h)).In subsection (a), before clause (1), the words “District of Columbia government” are added because of (ch. 182, ). In clause (1), the word “apportionment” is substituted for “apportionment or reapportionment” because of section 1512(a)(last sentence) of the revised title.section 9 of the Act of June 26, 191237 Stat. 184In subsection (b), the word “Mayor” is used because of Reorganization Plan No. 3 of 1967 (eff. , ) and sections 421, 422, and 771 of the District of Columbia Self-Government and Governmental Reorganization Act (, , 818). The word “President” is substituted for “President, through the Director of the Office of Management and Budget” because sections 101 and 102(a) of Reorganization Plan No. 2 of 1970 (eff. , ) designated the Bureau of the Budget as the Office of Management and Budget and transferred all functions of the Bureau to the President.Aug. 11, 1967July 1, 197081 Stat. 948Pub. Amendments2004—Subsec. (b). inserted at end “A copy of each report shall also be transmitted to the Comptroller General on the same date the report is transmitted to the President and Congress.”Pub. L. 108–447

children.text: the amount permitted by regulations prescribed under .section 1514(a) of this title

children.label: b
children.text: If an officer or employee of an executive agency or of the District of Columbia government violates subsection (a) of this section, the head of the executive agency or the Mayor of the District of Columbia, as the case may be, shall report immediately to the President and Congress all relevant facts and a statement of actions taken. A copy of each report shall also be transmitted to the Comptroller General on the same date the report is transmitted to the President and Congress.
